policy_statement: Budapest has set a target to reduce its greenhouse gas emissions by at least 40% by 2030 compared to the 2015 base year.
verbatim_text:
Accordingly, the Municipality of Budapest has set the target to reduce its emissions by 40% by 2030 compared to the base year of 2015.

Chart 2: The decarbonisation target of Budapest

2015: 6.11 million t CO2; 2030: -40%
section_header: 4.1.   REDUCTION OF THE EMISSION OF GREENHOUSE GASES (MITIGATION)
extraction_rationale: Quantified mitigation target with deadline and stated baseline year.
reasoning: The verbatim text contains an explicit, city-level mitigation outcome (“reduce its emissions by 40%”) with a clear deadline (“by 2030”) and a defined baseline (“base year of 2015”), plus supporting quantified context (“2015: 6.11 million t CO2; 2030: -40%”). This is an auditable, measurable commitment with spatial specificity (Municipality of Budapest). However, the excerpt does not explicitly state a binding legal mechanism (e.g., ordinance/mandate), so binding is marked “No”. Despite that, the language is firm (“has set the target to reduce”), not merely exploratory.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: “reduce its emissions by 40%” and “2030: -40%”. Timeline: “by 2030”. Spatial specificity: “Municipality of Budapest” indicates the jurisdiction covered. Binding mechanism: no explicit legal/mandatory instrument (no ordinance/requirement/code change) is cited in the excerpt, so marked No. Weak language: not framed as ‘encourage/explore/aim’; it states “has set the target to reduce”, which is a clear commitment. Strong language: contains an outcome with hard numbers (“40% by 2030”). Therefore it meets the strict rule (timeline Yes + quantifiable target Yes; weak language No), so VALID.

policy_statement: By joining the Under 2 Coalition, Budapest committed to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by at least 80% by 2050 compared to 1990 levels and/or reduce annual per-capita emissions below 2 metric tons.
verbatim_text: By joining the Coalition, the parties undertake to reduce their emission of greenhouse gases by 80% as a minimum by 2050 compared to the values in 1990 and/or reduce their annual per capita emission below 2 metric tons.
section_header: 4.1.   REDUCTION OF THE EMISSION OF GREENHOUSE GASES (MITIGATION)
extraction_rationale: Long-term quantified commitment (percentage reduction and/or per-capita threshold) with a deadline year.
reasoning: The verbatim text contains a quantified, economy-wide GHG commitment with an explicit deadline: “reduce their emission of greenhouse gases by **80% as a minimum by 2050 compared to the values in 1990** and/or **reduce their annual per capita emission below 2 metric tons**.” This is measurable and time-bound. However, the commitment is framed as “undertake” via joining a coalition, which suggests a pledge rather than a clearly enforceable local legal instrument; thus binding mechanism is not evidenced in the excerpt. Spatial applicability is implicit to “the parties” (i.e., the joining jurisdiction), but the text does not explicitly state “Budapest” or “citywide” in the verbatim snippet.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: Yes, because the text specifies measurable outcomes: “reduce their emission of greenhouse gases by 80% as a minimum” and “reduce their annual per capita emission below 2 metric tons.” Timeline: Yes, because it includes an explicit deadline: “by 2050.” Binding mechanism: No, because the excerpt describes a coalition pledge (“By joining the Coalition, the parties undertake to…”) and does not show an ordinance/mandate/code requirement. Spatial specificity: No, because the excerpt refers generically to “the parties” and does not explicitly name a geography (e.g., “Budapest,” “citywide,” or defined districts) within the verbatim text. Language strength: Strong language detected = Yes due to action verb with hard numbers (“undertake to reduce… by 2050… by 80%… and/or… below 2 metric tons”); Weak language detected = No (no ‘encourage/promote/explore/aim’ phrasing). STRICT VALID RULE: Passes because it is an auditable commitment with (has_timeline=Yes AND has_quantifiable_target=Yes), and weak_language_detected=No (binding mechanism not required in that case).

policy_statement: Carry out thematic awareness-raising campaigns and informational activities via the Mayor’s Office and Budapest business organisations, focusing on reducing household energy consumption, with annual financing of HUF 100 million and 2030 energy/CO2 targets.
verbatim_text:
Thematic  awareness-raising  campaigns  and  informational  activities  through  the Mayor's  Office  and  the business  organisations  of  Budapest,  with  special  regard  to reducing the volume of household energy consumption

| Necessary financing | HUF 100 million per annum |
| ... |
| Target year: 2030 | Target year value: 432,496 MWh; 85,744 tCO 2 /annum |
section_header: SZ-2 Climate conscious citizens: strengthening the environmental awareness and responsibility among the population and economic actors
extraction_rationale: This is a defined municipal measure (SZ5) with specified activities plus committed annual financing and quantified 2030 mitigation targets (MWh saved and tCO2/yr).
reasoning: The verbatim text describes a specific action (thematic awareness-raising campaigns and informational activities) delivered through named institutions (Mayor’s Office and Budapest business organisations) and focused on reducing household energy consumption. It includes spatial specificity (“Budapest”) and a clear timeline (“Target year: 2030”). It also provides quantifiable outcome targets for that target year (“432,496 MWh” and “85,744 tCO2/annum”), which makes the policy measurable. However, the language is programmatic and not explicitly binding (no ordinance/requirement indicated), so enforceability is unclear; still, the gold-standard measurability criteria are satisfied.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: The table specifies measurable outcomes: "Target year value: 432,496 MWh; 85,744 tCO2/annum". Timeline: The table provides an explicit deadline: "Target year: 2030". Binding mechanism: The text describes campaigns/activities but does not cite an enforceable mandate or legal requirement (no ordinance/regulation language), so 'No'. Spatial specificity: Implementation actors and geography are specified as "Mayor's Office" and "business organisations of Budapest" (Budapest). Weak language: Not framed as merely "encourage/explore/aim"; it states the activity will be carried out: "Thematic awareness-raising campaigns and informational activities...". Strong language: No explicit mandate/require/achieve phrasing with hard numbers; it is an activity description plus targets, so 'No'. STRICT VALID RULE: (1) Auditable commitment present (campaigns/activities to be conducted), (2) gold standard met because both timeline=Yes and quantifiable_target=Yes, (3) weak_language_detected=No, so passes -> VALID.

policy_statement: Develop the adaptation knowledge and ability of the population (heat waves, rainwater management, property protection, green infrastructure) through ongoing information/awareness actions, targeting 100,000 people reached by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Developing  the  adaptation  knowledge  and  ability  of  the  population,  particularly  in respect of heat waves, rainwater management, property protection and green infrastructure

| Indicator | Number of persons reached actively and passively |
| Base year: 2020 | Base year value: n.a. |
| Target year: 2030 | Target year value: 100 000 |
section_header: Developing the adaptation knowledge and ability of the population, particularly in respect of heat waves, rainwater management, property protection and green infrastructure
extraction_rationale: Concrete adaptation measure (SZ6) with an explicit quantified target (people reached by 2030) and described intervention focus areas.
reasoning: The statement specifies a concrete deliverable (“number of persons reached actively and passively”) with a quantified target value (100,000) and a clear deadline (target year 2030). It is spatially implicit (applies to “the population” without naming a jurisdiction in the excerpt). Language describes an implementation activity (“developing… through information/awareness actions”) rather than merely exploring, and it is measurable via the indicator table, though no explicit legal/binding instrument is cited.
validation_reasoning: Quantifiable target: Verbatim indicator table sets “Target year value: 100 000” for “Number of persons reached actively and passively.” Timeline: The table specifies “Target year: 2030.” Binding mechanism: No ordinance/mandate/regulatory instrument is mentioned in the excerpt, so enforceability cannot be confirmed. Spatial specificity: The text refers generally to “the population” without identifying a specific city/region/program area in the provided excerpt. Language strength/weakness: The policy is framed as delivery of awareness actions with an auditable KPI (persons reached) rather than aspirational verbs (no ‘encourage/explore/aim’); the KPI and target make it action-oriented and measurable.
